sub: Technology block (e): API tests everyone on the team can read

title: What Is Karate?
desc: An open-source test automation framework built for APIs and web services

items: Open-source tool for test automation, designed specifically for API tests and web services
Tests are written in a simple, readable syntax based on Gherkin — accessible even to non-programmers
Supports functional and performance tests, plus API, UI and end-to-end testing in one tool
Integrates quickly into CI/CD pipelines — known for efficiency and ease of use
For our domain: every UTM interface from Module 6/7 (clearance, telemetry, geo-zones) is a natural Karate target

title: Why We Test: Cover All Edge Cases
desc: Your collision-avoidance plugin will meet its stick-wielding tester in Module 12

body.alt: MonkeyUser comic: a robot builder is asked 'did you cover all edge cases?' — weeks later the robot wears a helmet, pillows and a roll cage
body.caption: monkeyuser.com — Edge Cases

title: Example 1: Testing a Greeting Endpoint
desc: A complete Karate feature file — no Java code required

body.code: Feature: greeting end-point Background: * url demoBaseUrl Scenario: get default greeting Given path 'greeting' When method get Then status 200 And match response == { id: '#number', content: 'Hello World!' } Scenario: get custom greeting Given path 'greeting' And param name = 'Billie' When method get Then status 200 And match response == { id: '#number', content: 'Hello Billie!' }
body.note: match validates the whole JSON payload structurally — '#number' is a fuzzy matcher: any numeric id passes.

title: Example 2: CSRF Token & Sign-In Flow
desc: Chaining requests: capture a token, then use it in the next request

body.code: Feature: csrf and sign-in end point Background: * url demoBaseUrl Given path 'signin', 'token' When method get Then status 200 And header X-CSRF-TOKEN = response Scenario: html url encoded form submit - post Given path 'signin' And form field username = 'john' And form field password = 'secret' When method post Then status 200 And match response == 'success'
body.note: The Background runs before each scenario: it fetches the CSRF token once and injects it as a header — state flows naturally between steps. The full demo also shows GET-based and manually-formed request variants.
